entries.title: Group Cruise Booking Process
entries.content: To book a group cruise through Cruise Brothers, contact the group desk of the specific cruise line directly using the Group phone number listed in the vendor directory. Most cruise lines define a group as 8+ cabins. Steps: (1) Call the cruise line's group desk to check availability and hold space. (2) Fill out the group booking form on cbagenttools.com under the Groups section. (3) Collect deposits from each passenger within the cruise line's deposit deadline. (4) Submit names and documentation to the cruise line. (5) Final payment is due per the cruise line's schedule. CB agents earn standard commission on group bookings plus potential group amenities like onboard credits, free berths, or cocktail parties depending on the cruise line and group size.

entries.title: Cruise Brothers Agent Perks and Benefits
entries.content: Cruise Brothers (CB) agents receive: (1) Competitive commission rates — typically 16% on major cruise lines, higher on luxury/river lines. (2) Access to exclusive CB pricing and promotions not available to the public. (3) CB Agent Tools portal (cbagenttools.com) with vendor contacts, marketing resources, booking forms, and training. (4) FAM (familiarization) trip opportunities at discounted or complimentary rates. (5) Override commissions for high producers. (6) Dedicated CB support team for booking assistance. (7) Marketing co-op funds and promotional materials. (8) Access to CB preferred rates with hotels and air vendors. (9) CLIA membership benefits if applicable. (10) Ongoing training webinars and cruise line certifications.

entries.title: CB Agent Tools Portal Overview
entries.content: The Cruise Brothers Agent Tools portal at cbagenttools.com is the primary resource hub for CB agents. It includes: (1) Vendor directory with commission rates and direct agent/group phone numbers. (2) Marketing materials, flyers, and promotional content. (3) Booking forms and group request forms. (4) Preferred vendor rate sheets. (5) Training resources and certification links. (6) CB announcements and promotions. (7) Contact information for CB support. Agents should use cbagenttools.com as their first stop for any booking or vendor question.

entries.title: Commission Structure and Payment
entries.content: Cruise Brothers agents earn commission on each completed booking. Commission is paid after the sailing date once the cruise line remits payment to CB. Standard commission rates range from 10% (Disney, Ritz-Carlton, budget lines) to 20% (Croisi Europe, luxury river lines). Most major ocean lines (Carnival, Royal Caribbean, Norwegian, Celebrity, Princess, Holland America) pay 16%. Luxury lines (Silversea, Oceania, Regent) pay 15-17%. Ultra-luxury (Seabourn, Crystal) pay 12-15%. Travel insurance vendors (Travel Guard, Allianz, Chubb) pay 10-38%. Commission is calculated on the base cruise fare, not taxes/port fees. Agents may be eligible for override commissions based on annual production volume.

entries.title: New Booking Process — Step by Step
entries.content: To make a new booking as a CB agent: (1) Identify the cruise line and sailing the client wants. (2) Call the cruise line's agent phone number (from the vendor directory) or book online through the cruise line's travel agent portal. (3) Provide your Cruise Brothers IATA/CLIA/TRUE number when booking — this ties the booking to CB for commission tracking. (4) Collect the client's deposit and submit it per the cruise line's requirements. (5) Record the booking confirmation number. (6) Monitor for final payment deadlines, document requirements, and ticketing dates. (7) CB commission posts after the sailing completes and the cruise line remits to CB. Always book under CB's agency credentials, never under a personal account.

entries.title: Cruise Brothers Contact and Support
entries.content: Cruise Brothers agent support is available through the cbagenttools.com portal. For general agent support, log into cbagenttools.com and use the contact or help section. For commission inquiries, escalate through the agent tools portal. For urgent booking issues, contact the specific cruise line's agent phone directly. CB does not have a single published agent support phone number that varies — all vendor-specific contacts are in the vendor directory. For tech issues with the CB portal, use the site's contact form.

entries.title: FAM Trip and Travel Agent Rate Policies
entries.content: Cruise Brothers agents may be eligible for FAM (familiarization) trips offered by cruise lines. Eligibility typically requires: minimum annual production (varies by cruise line, often $25k-$50k in sales), active CLIA or IATA membership, and CB approval. FAM trips are announced through cbagenttools.com and CB communications. Travel agent (TA) rates for personal travel are offered directly by cruise lines — agents should call the cruise line's agent phone, mention they are a travel agent, and ask about TA rates. Some cruise lines require proof of agency affiliation. TA rates are separate from FAM trips and available independent of production thresholds.

entries.title: Luxury Cruise Lines — Commission and Booking Notes
entries.content: Luxury and ultra-luxury lines have specific booking considerations for CB agents: Seabourn (12-15% commission): Call 800-929-9391 press 1. Regent Seven Seas (12-15%): Call 866-217-1371. Silversea (15%): Call 888-978-4070. Oceania (17%): Call 800-531-5658. Ritz-Carlton Yacht Collection (10%): Call 833-999-7292. Crystal Cruises (14%): Call 888-616-9615. These lines often have stricter deposit and cancellation policies. Many offer agent rates and FAM opportunities for qualifying producers. Luxury bookings tend to be higher-value and worth additional selling effort.

entries.title: River Cruise Lines — Commission and Booking Notes
entries.content: River cruise lines offer some of the highest commissions for CB agents: AmaWaterways (18%): Agent 800-626-0126, Group ext 1544 Lorena. Viking Cruises (18%): Agent 877.668.4546, Group 866-695-7128. Avalon Waterways (16%): Agent 866-270-9850, Group 800-221-0097. Emerald Cruises (18%): Agent 855-517-1200. Scenic Luxury Cruises (18%): Agent 855-517-1200. Croisi Europe (20% — highest): Agent 800-768-7232. Uniworld (18%): Agent 800-257-2407. River cruises are growing in popularity and typically appeal to older, affluent travelers. Groups on river cruises often get whole-ship charters or exclusive departures.

entries.title: Travel Insurance — Commission and Booking Notes
entries.content: Travel insurance is a high-margin add-on for CB agents. CB-preferred insurance vendors: Allianz Global (10-35% commission): Call 855-524-3687. Chubb Travel Insurance (10-38%): Call 844-772-3493. Travel Guard (30%): Call 866-729-5215. Agents should offer travel insurance on every booking. Insurance is typically booked through the cruise line's preferred provider or directly with the insurance company, citing your CB agency number. Trip cancellation, medical, and emergency evacuation are the key coverages to highlight. Insurance commissions post separately from cruise commissions.
